summarization.a: Multi-axis chart showing the dependence of growth per cycle (left axis) and silicon atomic content (right axis) on SiH₄ pulse time during plasma-enhanced ALD of Ti–Si–N at 350 °C. Both quantities increase rapidly at short pulse times and saturate for SiH₄ exposures of ~8 s or longer, reaching ~0.7 Å/cycle growth and ~9–15 at.% Si, indicating self-limiting surface reactions.

a.question: How do the growth per cycle and silicon content change as the SiH₄ pulse time increases from 0 to ~8 s?
a.answer: Both the growth per cycle and the silicon content increase rapidly as the SiH₄ pulse time is extended from 0 to about 8 s. This reflects increased surface reaction completion during the SiH₄ exposure. Beyond this range, further increases in pulse time do not significantly change either quantity.

a.question: Does extending the SiH₄ pulse time beyond ~8 s significantly increase the film growth rate?
Why does the silicon atomic percentage stop increasing once the growth per cycle has saturated?
a.answer: Once the surface reactive sites available for Si incorporation are fully occupied during the SiH₄ half-cycle, additional precursor exposure cannot introduce more silicon. This reflects the self-limiting surface chemistry characteristic of ALD, where adsorption and reaction terminate once saturation is reached.

a.question: What is the shortest SiH₄ pulse time required to achieve a growth per cycle of approximately 0.7 Å/cycle?
